quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)

relations.note: Body 16px/24px equals the 150% minimum line height requirement.
The defined scale supports the required hierarchy distinction.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Body copy | **Nunito Sans**
'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it

rules.intent: Ensure consistent, medium-appropriate typeface usage with reliable email fallback.
rules.rule_text: Typography families are medium-dependent: headlines/headers use Agenda in print (headline.font = Agenda) and Nunito Sans in digital/email with bolder weights and Title Case; body copy, footers and fine print use Nunito Sans (body.font); Word/PPT/Excel fallback is Aptos. Agenda may be used as live text in digital ONLY for the campaign headline (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). The email CSS font stack (font.email.stack = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif) must be used, with Nunito Sans loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported and the stack guaranteeing graceful fallback in Outlook.

quotes: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
use sparingly, never for primary content
must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Maintain a consistent, legible email type hierarchy with an enforced minimum size.
rules.rule_text: Email type scale (px / line-height): H1 section hero headline 28px/36px (≈130%) Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold; H2 section heading 22px/30px (≈136%) Nunito Sans Bold; H3 subhead 18px/26px (≈144%) Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold; Body 16px/24px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular, with 16px the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ('bigger is better'); secondary/supporting body 14px/21px (150%) Nunito Sans Regular used sparingly and never for primary content; footnote/reference text within a section 12px/18px (150%) that must be 'noticeably smaller' than primary data and clearly separated from it. Never set running text below 12px (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component).

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Preserve legibility of body copy, especially light-on-dark.
rules.rule_text: Body copy line height is a minimum of 150% (body.line_height = 150%) as a brand accessibility rule; for light text on dark backgrounds, increase further to 160% (line_height.light_on_dark = 160%, e.g. 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Ensure readable hierarchy and prevent ambiguous ranking of adjacent text.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
